entry_a.title: Create worktrees atomically
entry_a.content: Worktree creation must be a single atomic operation — if it fails partway, roll back the mkdir and the git worktree add must not leave the main checkout in a broken state. Use a finalize/rollback pattern.

entry_b.title: Clean up worktrees after merge
entry_b.content: Once an issue's PR merges, the associated worktree must be removed and its branch pruned. Stale worktrees accumulate disk space and confuse the orchestrator's active-worker state.

notes: Same domain (worktrees) but different rules — atomic creation vs. cleanup on merge. Merging would lose both specifics.
